- "[[Ministry of Mines and Mining Development]], [[Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP)]]"

The **Aviation Approval**, issued by the **[[Civil Aviation Authority of Zimbabwe (CAAZ)]]** under the **Civil Aviation Act**, serves as the official legal and technical clearance required to construct and operate private or commercial airstrips in Zimbabwe. It certifies that the physical design, runway alignment, and geographical coordinates of the facility comply with international safety standards, ensuring that it will not conflict with existing commercial flight paths or restricted military airspace. Functioning as a critical checkpoint before physical construction begins, this approval validates the structural layout for aviation safety and requires a prior **EIA Certificate** along with background security clearances from the Ministry of Defense to protect national airspace integrity.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]
**Issuing Entity**: [[Registrar of Companies and Other Business Entities]]
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Courier Operator Licence** issued by the **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** under the [[Postal and Telecommunications Act]] Chapter 12:05 authorises entities to provide courier and parcel‑delivery services across the country, ensuring that operators meet stringent standards for security, reliability, and consumer protection; the licence mandates compliance with regulated pricing structures, tracking and reporting requirements, and adherence to national and international postal conventions, thereby fostering a trustworthy and accountable logistics sector that supports both commercial and personal communications while safeguarding the integrity of the national postal network.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Data Service Provider Licence** is a regulatory authorization granted by the **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** under the provisions of the **[[Postal and Telecommunications Act]] [Chapter 12:05]**, which permits entities to offer data transmission, internet access, and related digital communication services within Zimbabwe; the licence obliges holders to comply with technical standards, consumer protection rules, and spectrum management policies, while also ensuring that their operations contribute to the development of the national telecommunications infrastructure and adhere to the authority’s licensing conditions and reporting requirements.

The **Diamond Marketing Approval** is a formal authorization granted by the **Minerals Marketing Corporation of Zimbabwe (MMCZ)** under the provisions of the **[[MMCZ Act]]**, which certifies that a diamond has been sourced, processed, and is ready for export in compliance with national regulations; this approval ensures that each stone meets the stringent quality, traceability, and ethical standards set by the corporation, thereby facilitating lawful trade, protecting the country’s mineral resources, and maintaining Zimbabwe’s reputation in the global diamond market.

The **Distribution Licence** issued by **Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA)** under the **[[Electricity Act]]** is a regulatory instrument that authorises an entity to own, operate, maintain and develop the electricity distribution network within a defined service area, ensuring that the infrastructure meets safety, reliability and quality standards; it obliges the licensee to adhere to tariff frameworks, performance benchmarks and consumer protection provisions set by the Authority, while also requiring compliance with technical specifications, reporting duties and investment commitments aimed at expanding access and enhancing the efficiency of the power supply system across Zimbabwe.

The **EIA Certificate** is an official approval issued by the **Environmental Management Agency (EMA)** pursuant to the **[[Environmental Management Act]]**, confirming that a proposed development has undergone a comprehensive Environmental Impact Assessment and meets all statutory requirements for environmental protection; the certificate details the specific mitigation measures, monitoring obligations, and compliance timelines that the project must adhere to, thereby ensuring that potential adverse effects on ecosystems, public health, and natural resources are minimized and managed throughout the lifecycle of the activity.

The **Employer Registration** issued by the **National Social Security Authority (NSSA)** under the **[[NSSA Act]]** is a mandatory process that formally records an employer’s legal and operational details with the authority, enabling the organization to enroll its workforce in the national social security scheme, remit contributions, and access statutory benefits; it requires submission of verified corporate documents, a completed registration form, and compliance with stipulated timelines, after which the employer receives a unique registration number that serves as the reference for all subsequent interactions with the NSSA, ensuring accountability and streamlined administration of social security obligations.

The **Environmental Management Agency (EMA)** issues an Environmental Approval under the **[[Environmental Management Act]]** to formally certify that a proposed project or activity complies with all applicable environmental standards, regulations, and mitigation requirements; this approval confirms that the applicant has conducted thorough assessments, incorporated necessary safeguards to protect ecosystems, and secured any required conditions or monitoring plans, thereby granting legal permission to proceed while ensuring that environmental impacts are minimized and managed in accordance with national policy objectives.

The **Environmental Management Agency (EMA)** issues an Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in accordance with the **[[Environmental Management Act]]**, a comprehensive regulatory framework that mandates a systematic evaluation of potential environmental consequences before any major project proceeds. The EIA process, overseen by **EMA**, requires developers to identify, predict, and mitigate adverse effects on ecosystems, water quality, air emissions, and community health, while also considering cumulative impacts and alternative project designs. By integrating scientific analysis, stakeholder consultation, and transparent reporting, the assessment ensures that decision‑makers have a robust evidence base to balance economic development with the preservation of natural resources and the well‑being of current and future generations.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Ministry of Mines and Mining Development]]

The **Exclusive Prospecting Order** is a regulatory instrument issued by the **Ministry of Mines and Mining Development** under the provisions of the **[[Mines and Minerals Act]]**, granting a designated applicant the sole right to explore a specified mineral resource area for a defined period; it delineates the scope of permissible activities, obliges the holder to adhere to environmental and safety standards, and requires the submission of detailed work programmes and financial assurances, thereby ensuring that the exploration is conducted responsibly while protecting the state’s mineral assets and promoting sustainable development.

The **Explosives Permit** issued by the **Zimbabwe Republic Police** under the **[[Explosives Act]]** is a regulatory authorization that allows individuals, companies, or organizations to possess, store, transport, manufacture, or use explosive materials for legitimate purposes such as mining, construction, or public safety; the permit outlines strict conditions regarding the security of storage facilities, the qualifications of personnel handling the explosives, and the documentation required for each transaction, and it mandates periodic inspections and compliance reporting to ensure that all activities conform to national safety standards and legal obligations, thereby safeguarding public welfare while facilitating controlled industrial and commercial use of hazardous substances.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Fidelity Gold Refinery]]; [[Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ)]]
**Issuing Entity**: [[Fidelity Gold Refinery]]; [[Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ)]]

The Export Authorization issued by **Fidelity Gold Refinery** and the **Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ)** under the [[Gold Trade Regulations]] is a formal permit that confirms the refinery’s compliance with Zimbabwe’s stringent controls on gold extraction, processing, and shipment, guaranteeing that each consignment meets the country’s quality, provenance and anti‑money‑laundering standards; the document records the volume, purity and destination of the gold, authorizes its legal export, and serves as a traceable record for customs and financial oversight, thereby facilitating transparent trade while protecting the nation’s mineral resources and revenue streams.

The **Minerals Marketing Corporation of Zimbabwe (MMCZ)** issues an Export Permit under the **[[MMCZ Act]]** to authorize the lawful shipment of mineral commodities from Zimbabwe to foreign markets; this permit confirms that the applicant has met all statutory requirements, including proof of ownership, compliance with quality and grading standards, payment of applicable royalties and taxes, and adherence to environmental and safety regulations, thereby ensuring that the export is conducted transparently, responsibly, and in alignment with national policy objectives aimed at maximizing revenue and promoting sustainable resource development.

The **Forestry Commission** issues a Forestry Permit under the **[[Forestry Act]]** to authorize the lawful use, management, and exploitation of forest lands, ensuring that activities such as timber harvesting, planting, and conservation are conducted in accordance with sustainable practices, environmental safeguards, and statutory requirements; the permit delineates the specific area, duration, and conditions of operation, mandates compliance with best‑practice guidelines, and obliges permit holders to submit regular monitoring reports, thereby balancing economic interests with the protection of biodiversity and the long‑term health of the forest ecosystem.

The **Fuel Storage Licence** is a regulatory permit granted by the **Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA)** under the **[[Mineral and Petroleum Resources Development Act]]** framework, authorising entities to construct, operate, and maintain facilities for the safe storage of petroleum products such as gasoline, diesel, kerosene and lubricants. The licence ensures that storage installations meet stringent technical, environmental and safety standards, including fire‑prevention measures, spill containment, and regular inspections, thereby protecting public health, the environment and the integrity of the national fuel supply chain. Holders must comply with reporting obligations, maintain up‑to‑date operational records, and undergo periodic audits to retain the licence, which can be revoked or suspended for non‑compliance or safety breaches.

The **Generation Licence** issued by the **Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA)** under the [[Electricity Act]] is a regulatory instrument that authorises an entity to develop, construct, operate, and maintain electricity‑generating facilities within Zimbabwe, ensuring that the proposed project meets technical, environmental, and financial standards; it obliges the holder to comply with grid‑connection requirements, adhere to prescribed safety and performance criteria, and submit regular reports on output and compliance, while also granting ZERA the authority to monitor, enforce conditions, and, if necessary, suspend or revoke the licence to protect the reliability and sustainability of the national power system.

The **Gold Buying / Delivery Registration** issued by **Fidelity Gold Refinery** under the **[[Gold Trade Regulations]]** serves as a formal record that authorizes the refinery to purchase, receive, and dispatch gold on behalf of licensed traders, ensuring that each transaction complies with statutory purity, weight, and provenance standards; the registration requires detailed documentation of the buyer’s credentials, the source and assay of the metal, and a traceable delivery schedule, thereby providing regulatory bodies with transparent oversight while safeguarding market integrity and preventing illicit trade.

The **Hazardous Substance Licence** is a regulatory permit granted by the **Environmental Management Agency (EMA)** under the **[[Environmental Management Act]]** that authorises businesses and individuals to store, handle, transport, or dispose of hazardous substances in compliance with strict safety and environmental standards; applicants must demonstrate robust risk‑management plans, secure containment facilities, and ongoing monitoring procedures, while the licence imposes conditions such as regular reporting, emergency response protocols, and periodic inspections to ensure that the handling of dangerous chemicals does not jeopardise public health or ecological integrity.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Internet Access Provider (IAP) Licence** is a regulatory instrument granted by the **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** under the provisions of the [[Postal and Telecommunications Act ]] [Chapter 12:05]; it authorises entities to offer internet connectivity services to the public, stipulates compliance with technical standards, consumer protection measures, and spectrum usage rules, and requires licence holders to maintain service quality, report operational data, and adhere to licensing fees and renewal conditions, thereby ensuring a structured and accountable framework for the development of Zimbabwe’s broadband ecosystem.

The **Investment License** issued by the **Zimbabwe Investment and Development Agency (ZIDA)** under the **[[Zimbabwe Investment and Development Agency Act]]** serves as the principal authorisation for both domestic and foreign investors to establish, expand, or restructure businesses in Zimbabwe, granting them preferential treatment such as tax incentives, duty exemptions, and streamlined regulatory procedures; it confirms that the proposed venture aligns with national development priorities, complies with sector‑specific regulations, and meets the agency’s criteria for economic impact, job creation, and technology transfer, thereby providing a legal framework that facilitates investment while ensuring accountability and transparency in the country’s growth agenda.

The **Minerals Marketing Corporation of Zimbabwe (MMCZ)** issues a Mineral Marketing Registration under the **[[MMCZ Act]]** as a compulsory licence that authorises mining entities to legally market, sell, and export their mineral products through the corporation’s regulated channels; the registration confirms that the holder has complied with all statutory requirements, including proof of ownership, environmental compliance, and payment of applicable royalties, thereby ensuring transparent trade, safeguarding national revenue, and facilitating access to both domestic and international markets while maintaining strict oversight of the country’s mineral resources.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Ministry of Mines and Mining Development]]

The **Ministry of Mines and Mining Development** issues a Mining Lease, also known as a Mining Title, under the provisions of the **[[Mines and Minerals Act]]**, granting the holder exclusive rights to explore, develop, and extract specified mineral resources within a defined geographic area; the lease delineates the scope of permissible activities, obligates the lessee to adhere to environmental and safety standards, mandates the payment of royalties and fees, and includes provisions for renewal, suspension, or cancellation should the holder fail to meet statutory requirements or the terms of the agreement.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** issues a Network Facilities Licence under the [[Postal and Telecommunications Act]] to authorize the construction, operation, and maintenance of telecommunications infrastructure such as fiber‑optic cables, microwave links, and related transmission equipment; the licence ensures that operators meet technical standards, adhere to spectrum management rules, and comply with service quality, safety, and security obligations, while also providing a framework for the allocation of rights‑of‑way, interconnection agreements, and the protection of consumer interests, thereby fostering a competitive and reliable communications environment across Zimbabwe.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** issues a Postal Operator Licence under the **[[Postal and Telecommunications Act]] (Chapter 12:05)**, which authorises entities to provide a full range of postal services—including the collection, sorting, transport, and delivery of letters, parcels, and other mail items—within Zimbabwe and across international borders; the licence also mandates compliance with standards for service quality, security, and consumer protection, requires the holder to maintain appropriate financial and technical capacity, and obliges regular reporting to **POTRAZ** to ensure that the postal network operates efficiently, reliably, and in alignment with national regulatory objectives.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Zimbabwe Investment and Development Agency (ZIDA)]]; [[Ministry of Finance, Economic Development and Investment Promotion]]
**Issuing Entity**: [[Zimbabwe Investment and Development Agency (ZIDA)]]; [[Ministry of Finance, Economic Development and Investment Promotion]]
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Private Network Licence** is a regulatory instrument granted by the **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** under the **[[Postal and Telecommunications Act]] [Chapter 12:05]**, authorising entities to build, operate and maintain closed, non‑public telecommunications infrastructures such as corporate intranets, data‑centres and specialised radio networks; the licence imposes strict compliance requirements on spectrum usage, interconnection standards and service quality, while also mandating that licence‑holders ensure their networks do not interfere with public telecommunications services and that they adhere to the authority’s monitoring, reporting and security protocols.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Ministry of Mines and Mining Development]]

The **Prospecting Licence** is a statutory authorization granted by the **Ministry of Mines and Mining Development** under the provisions of the **[[Mines and Minerals Act]]**, allowing individuals or companies to explore a designated area for the presence of mineral resources; the licence delineates the geographic boundaries, duration, and permissible activities, obliges the holder to submit regular exploration reports, adhere to environmental and safety standards, and pay the requisite fees, while also providing the government with a mechanism to monitor and regulate mineral prospecting activities to ensure sustainable resource development and compliance with national mining policy.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Public Telecommunications Service Licence (PTSL)** is a regulatory instrument granted by the **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** under the provisions of the [[Postal and Telecommunications Act]]  [Chapter 12:05]; it authorises entities to operate public telecommunications networks and provide services such as voice, data, and internet access to the general public, subject to compliance with technical standards, quality of service obligations, and financial and reporting requirements designed to promote competition, protect consumer interests, and ensure the sustainable development of Zimbabwe’s telecommunications infrastructure.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** issues a Radio Station Licence pursuant to the **[[[Postal and Telecommunications Act]] [Chapter 12:05]**, which authorises broadcasters to operate legally within the country’s spectrum framework; the licence sets out the technical parameters, content standards, and compliance obligations that applicants must meet, including the allocation of frequencies, adherence to national broadcasting codes, payment of applicable fees, and ongoing reporting requirements, thereby ensuring that radio services are delivered in a manner that promotes fair competition, protects public interest, and aligns with Zimbabwe’s regulatory policies for telecommunications and media.

The **National Railways of Zimbabwe (NRZ)** issues a Rail Access Approval under the provisions of the **[[Railways Act]]** to grant third‑party operators the legal right to use its railway infrastructure for the movement of goods or passengers; the approval delineates the specific routes, time slots, safety standards, and fee structures applicable to the applicant, while also imposing obligations such as compliance with NRZ’s operational rules, insurance requirements, and liability provisions, thereby ensuring that all rail traffic is coordinated, secure, and consistent with national transportation policy.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Ministry of Mines and Mining Development]]

The **Registration of Mine** is an official document issued by the **Ministry of Mines and Mining Development** pursuant to the **[[Mines and Minerals Act]]**, confirming that a mining operation has met all statutory requirements and is legally authorized to explore, extract, and process mineral resources within a defined area; the registration details the mine’s location, ownership, scope of activities, and compliance obligations, and serves as the primary legal instrument that enables the holder to obtain necessary permits, secure financing, and engage with stakeholders while ensuring adherence to environmental, safety, and revenue‑sharing regulations stipulated by the Act.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Zimbabwe National Road Administration (ZINARA)]]; Local Authority

The **SEZ Investor Approval** is a formal authorization granted by the **Zimbabwe Investment and Development Agency (ZIDA)** under the provisions of the **[[Zimbabwe Investment and Development Agency Act]]**, confirming that a proposed investment meets all regulatory, fiscal and strategic criteria for operation within a designated Special Economic Zone; the approval process involves a comprehensive review of the project’s economic impact, compliance with zoning regulations, and alignment with national development objectives, after which the agency issues a certificate that enables the investor to access preferential tax regimes, streamlined customs procedures and other incentives designed to foster export‑oriented growth and job creation in Zimbabwe’s priority sectors.
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]

The **Spectrum Licence** issued by the **Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)** under the **[[Postal and Telecommunications Act]]** is a regulatory instrument that authorises the allocation and use of specific radio frequency bands to telecommunications operators, broadcasters, and other service providers within Zimbabwe; it sets out the technical parameters, duration, fees, and compliance obligations required to ensure efficient spectrum management, prevent interference, and promote fair competition while supporting the country’s broader objectives of expanding connectivity, fostering innovation, and safeguarding national security.
**Legislation**: [[Public Procurement and Disposal of Public Assets Act]]
**Issuing Entity**: [[Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ)]]
